weather.summary: Saturday: 6°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 13°C, scattered clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun things to do with your little one! I've found free museum activities, outdoor adventures, and even a chance to see some animals. Get ready for a fun-filled weekend!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed environment for toddlers to explore books and stories. Perfect for a rainy day.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: Two weeks of Easter activities in Victoria Park
activities.description: A full fortnight of free, daily activities including sports and fishing.
activities.whyItWorks: There are one to two major activities a day. For a schedule, details of start points and the activities you do have to book for, you're best heading to the Victoria Park Facebook page.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Wick, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check Victoria Park Facebook page for schedule.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Museum dedicated to childhood creativity, with collections of toys, games, clothes and art.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of interactive exhibits and sensory play areas suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check out the sensory garden

activities.title: Mome London – Creative Play & Café
activities.description: A creative play café and family hub in Angel, Islington, designed especially for babies and toddlers aged.
activities.whyItWorks: A safe and stimulating environment for free play with coffee and snacks available for parents.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Check opening times before you go!

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Community farm with animals, gardens, and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers will love seeing the animals up close, and there's space to run around.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle or bus
transport.tip: Check out the farm shop for fresh produce.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore historical homes through the ages. Step into historical homes through the ages, which may sound boring but is, in fact, pretty fun for kids. There are lots of hands-on things for them to do, even an old computer with basic games like the original Sims and pinball. The Museum of the Home also has a beautiful little, enclosed garden, so it's a perfect museum to visit in spring or summer.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and a beautiful garden.  Pick up an activity sheet or a free explorer backpack from the front desk.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hoxton, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check out the enclosed garden

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed environment for toddlers to explore books and stories. Perfect for a rainy day.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: East London's Victoria Park
activities.description: East London's most famous park is well known for the adult festivals it hosts in the summer, but over Easter it practically has its own festival for little ones, with a full fortnight of free, daily, mostly no booking required activities that stretch from sports and fishing (!) on Tuesday March 31 through to pond dipping on Friday April 10.
activities.whyItWorks: The East London's most famous park is well known for the adult festivals it hosts in the summer, but over Easter it practically has its own festival for little ones, with a full fortnight of free, daily, mostly no booking required activities.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Wick, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check Victoria Park Facebook page for schedule.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Wonderlab offers a great hands-on experience for the kids with experiment demonstrations, talks, activities and educational play zones.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book Wonderlab in advance to avoid disappointment.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth. Step inside the Natural History Museum and you'll be greeted by a 25.2-metre-long blue whale skeleton suspended from the ceiling of Hintze Hall. From here, children can explore more than 80 million objects spanning 4.5 billion years, including dinosaur fossils and meteorites.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and dinosaur fossils that toddlers will love. The Natural History Museum hosts Easter-themed workshops, interactive exhibits, and family-friendly activities.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaur gallery

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: The British Museum has on display fabulous goodies from the ancient world – Assyria, Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece and Rome, plus prehistoric Europe.
activities.whyItWorks: Visiting here is cultural and educational while still being fun for children. When you arrive, visit the family desk. Here you can get a selection of activity sheets and also borrow an Explorer Backpack.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then the Central Line to Tottenham Court Road.
transport.tip: Pick up an Explorer Backpack

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo in a park setting, with a variety of animals including birds, goats, and deer.
activities.whyItWorks: Free and easily accessible, perfect for a short visit with toddlers who love animals.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine the zoo visit with a walk in the park.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Magical playground inspired by Peter Pan, with a huge wooden pirate ship, teepees, and sensory trails.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of play equipment suitable for different ages and abilities. Ideal for active toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then the Central Line to Queensway or Notting Hill Gate.
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid queues.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic views of London.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers stunning views and a unique experience. There are restaurants and cafes available for snacks.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Circle Line to Monument.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to guarantee entry.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Battersea Park offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.
activities.whyItWorks: Battersea Park offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the boating lake.

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: A heartwarming show about finding your roar.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for 3-year-olds, this show is engaging and age-appropriate.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Northern Line to London Bridge, then the Jubilee Line to Southwark.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: Planet Omar
activities.description: Show at Unicorn Theatre.
activities.whyItWorks: A great family show for older children

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Northern Line to London Bridge, then the Jubilee Line to Southwark.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: Toto Kerblammo!
activities.description: Show at Unicorn Theatre.
activities.whyItWorks: A great family show for older children

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Northern Line to London Bridge, then the Jubilee Line to Southwark.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.description: Enjoy family fun with amazing shows, dancing, puppetry and free events this Easter.Also worth visiting is the REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Wide range of activities to suit all interests and ages.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Northern Line to Embankment or the Jubilee Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the full programme.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: The musical adaptation of the classic children's book.
activities.whyItWorks: A heartwarming story with catchy songs and colourful costumes.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Central Line to Holborn, then the Piccadilly Line to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: Uniqlo Tate Play: Make Studio: Memory
activities.dates: Wed, Sat, Sun and holidays. Until 22 Jul
activities.description: Step into a space for remembering, imagining and sharing
activities.whyItWorks: u5 on Wed, all ages weekends, Free, drop-in.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Jubilee line to Southwark.
transport.tip: Check out the UNIQLO Tate Play website.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.price: Adult £15.50, child £13.50. All tickets £8.50 at 4.45pm on Thur, and at 4.45pm on 18 Mar. All tickets £10 at 4.45pm on Fri.
activities.description: Show. Heartwarming and hilarious adventure.
activities.whyItWorks: The show is heartwarming and hilarious adventure.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or overground to Angel station.
transport.tip: Book in advance to avoid disapointment

activities.title: Playing Shakespeare with Deutsche Bank: Romeo and Juliet
activities.description: Critically acclaimed 90-min version created especially for young people
activities.whyItWorks: A great family show for older children. Plus Romeo & Juliet Family Workshop (9-11) 28 Mar-11 Apr, £10.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge
transport.tip: Family workshops for older kids.
